comment.body:
Hi Alma, I just go back to watch your weekly post and have an interpretation about why MSFT goes up today, and want to ask you if my thoughts are correct or not. 
So MSFT today's RV change is -3.86%, means RV goes -3.86% from yesterday, I assume CTAs use yesterday's RV as Vol(target), today's RV as Vol(current) and Vol(asset), that make MSFT's exposure multiplier > 1 and Weight(asset) goes up, which resulting today's buying. 
Am I right about it? please correct me if I am wrong
comment.raw_body:
Hi Alma, I just go back to watch your weekly post and have an interpretation about why MSFT goes up today, and want to ask you if my thoughts are correct or not. 
So MSFT today's RV change is -3.86%, means RV goes -3.86% from yesterday, I assume CTAs use yesterday's RV as Vol(target), today's RV as Vol(current) and Vol(asset), that make MSFT's exposure multiplier > 1 and Weight(asset) goes up, which resulting today's buying. 
Am I right about it? please correct me if I am wrong

comment.body:
so if you look at the chart, there was a sudden negativ gamma there, 10 pts below the 6145 long gamma bar. the speed was locally negative there, steeple decerased above 6100, suddenly and steep.
this creates a net suppressive effect there, a magnet from the 6100 bar, and the local effect at 6130/35 is similar to a vanna flip, but it wasn't that.

comment.body: FWIW dept.  --  with trading you don't want to get too predictive, but this is the general path i see toward a final top to the market.  There would be 3 new ATH's ending in the TOP.  The two down legs shown would be very sharp, possibly with gaps down.  I don't expect anyone to believe it.  It involves a pattern I've used for years in top formations.  like i said, FWIW
